title: Prehistoric art
topic_path: Test your knowledge about prehistoric art

hints:
"Paleo" is from the Greek *palaios* (which means "old") and "lithic" is from Greek *lithikos* (which means "stone").

[Read this brief essay to learn more](/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-c-e--ancient-cultures-1/prehistoric/a/prehistoric-art-paleolithic-origins)

choices.content: The word "Paleolithic" comes from Latin and means "a really long time ago."
The word "Paleolithic" comes from Arabic and means "before writing."
The word "Paleolithic" comes from Greek and means "old stone age."
The word "Paleolithic" comes from the Hebrew word meaning "hard stone."

assessment_items.question_content:
**Prehistoric artists wrote down their thoughts, feelings, and artistic process for later generations of artists and others to read.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Prehistoric people did not have written language.

[Read this brief essay to learn more](/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-c-e--ancient-cultures-1/prehistoric/a/prehistoric-art-paleolithic-origins)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Humans were making art long before they invented writing or farming.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Writing and farming are relatively recent developments from the Neolithic period—human beings were making art long before.

[Read this brief essay to learn more](/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-c-e--ancient-cultures-1/prehistoric/a/the-neolithic-revolution)

assessment_items.question_content:
**What did the prehistoric paintings in European caves mean to the people who created them?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Prehistoric people didn't leave behind written records, so therefore there is no way to determine with certainty what they meant.

[Read this brief essay to learn more](/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-c-e--ancient-cultures-1/prehistoric/a/prehistoric-art-paleolithic-origins)

choices.content: The cave paintings are evidence of a prehistoric religion.
The cave paintings are evidence of a prehistoric political system.
The cave paintings show how much people cared about animals.

assessment_items.question_content:
**The prehistoric figurines that represent humans are almost all male.**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The prehistoric figurines that represent humans are almost all female.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What does the size of the *Venus of Willendorf* suggest about the people that made her?**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b6492b49f331d35df02649ae033f9341.jpeg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The *Venus of Willendorf* was made by people who moved around a lot and didn't use wheeled carts when they travelled. The figure is 4-3/8" (or 11.1 cm) high.

choices.content: The *Venus of Willendorf* is small and fits into the palm of your hand. The people that made the sculpture were nomadic, which means that they moved to different locations throughout the year, and did not stay in the same place.

The *Venus of Willendorf* is a giant statue (as seen in the movie *Hellboy 2*). People probably worshipped this sculpture and gave her offerings of food. These people lived in the same place over a long period of time.
The *Venus of Willendorf* is as small as a bead, so that she could be worn on a necklace by women.
The *Venus of Willendorf* is the size of a newborn, which shows that she reminded people about fertility and the goddess Venus.
